neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Lowertown
neighborhoods.description: Historic state capital with Lowertown artist lofts, Mears Park, and the renovated Union Depot
neighborhoods.character: State capital, artist lofts, historic depot

housing.predominantType: Converted warehouse lofts and modern apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Saint Paul. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Saint Paul

neighborhoods.name: Highland Park
neighborhoods.description: South-of-the-river neighborhood with Highland Village shopping, Ford site redevelopment, and river access
neighborhoods.character: Highland Village, Ford site redevelopment, river

housing.predominantType: Colonial houses, bungalows, and newer condos

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Saint Paul's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Saint Paul

neighborhoods.name: East Side / Payne-Phalen
neighborhoods.description: Diverse eastern neighborhoods with Phalen Lake, Hmong markets, and affordable housing
neighborhoods.character: Diverse, Phalen Lake, ethnic markets, affordable

housing.predominantType: Bungalows, modest houses, and affordable apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Saint Paul

neighborhoods.name: Roseville / Maplewood
neighborhoods.description: Northern suburbs with Rosedale Center, family parks, and established residential areas
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Saint Paul
